_description: Pre-computed model lookup tables for dashboard integration without Python runtime
_updated: Weather-adjusted + drop-date-aware DM model. Incorporates 2026 actual data through 3/22, DM drop schedule, and weather impact analysis.

weather_multipliers._note: Lead multiplier vs typical weekday baseline. Apply to seasonal baseline to get adjusted forecast.

dow_multipliers._note: Day-of-week multiplier vs weekday average. Updated with 2026 actuals: Saturday and Sunday are higher than original 5Y model due to DM weekend response and digital channel growth. Blended from 5Y historical + 2026 actual weights.

seasonal_baseline_weekly._note: ORGANIC-ONLY weekday baseline (excludes DM). When dm_waves_2026 is available, use wave-based DM calculation instead of dm_addon_weekly. These baselines are 5Y averages; apply weather multipliers for actual conditions.

dm_addon_weekly._note: LEGACY: Average DM leads per weekday when mail is in home (5Y historical average). SUPERSEDED by dm_waves_2026 when specific drop data is available. Kept as fallback for dates outside known waves.

dm_waves_2026._note: 2026 DM drop schedule modeled as paired waves. Each wave = two drops 2 days apart. In-home date is first drop + 3 days. Response window is 14 days from wave in-home start. Weights are % of total wave response per day (sum to 100). Based on wave 1 actual data (608 DM leads from 400K pieces, 3/6-3/19). Wave 2 total is estimated higher (+20%) because it lands during Peak season with warmer weather (validated: 2026 organic was +22% in Peak vs Ramp).

waves.weather_context: Ramp phase. Wave 1 response began during recovering temps after week 9 cold snap (34°F avg high, 0.48in snow). Response started strong on Mon 3/9 as temps climbed above 40°F.
Peak phase. Warmer temps and higher base demand amplify DM response. 2026 week 11 organic was +22% vs 2025 showing strong underlying demand.

response_curve_pct._note: Day-by-day % of total wave DM response (days 0-13 from wave in-home start). Derived from 2026 wave 1 actuals. Reflects combined DOW + response timing shape. Apply DOW multiplier and weather multiplier on top for forecast.

temperature_impact._note: Lead volume relative to baseline by temperature range. Validated by 2026: Week 9 at 34.4°F with snow produced -69% organic vs 2025 (53°F no snow). The below_40 + snow compound effect is severe in Early/Ramp phases.

weather_context_2026._note: Weather conditions during 2026 season that explain lead patterns.

week_9_cold_snap.impact: Organic leads -69% vs 2025 same week (160 vs 513). Coldest week 9 in 5 years. Snow on ground (0.48in avg) + highs below 35°F created compound suppression during Early phase when weather sensitivity is very high.

dm_timing_shift.impact: 2025 had DM active from week 8 (163 DM leads wk8, 168 wk9). 2026 first drop was 3/3 (in-home 3/6), leaving weeks 8-9 with zero DM. Combined with cold snap, this accounts for ~85% of the 1,934-lead gap vs 2025 through Mar 22.

recovery_signal.impact: Week 11 organic (911) was +22% vs 2025 (749) and +6% vs 2024 (861). Once temps recovered and DM activated, organic response was strong. Suggests baseline demand is healthy when weather cooperates.
